provenance.source_corpus: Divergent Pathways of Successional Recovery for In Situ Oil Sands Exploration Drilling Pads on Wooded Moderate‐Rich Fens in Alberta, Canada

source_spans.text: OSE pad preparation modifies banks of vegetative propagules and seeds, exposes lower layers of non-decomposed peat, and reduces variation in microtopography and microhabitats available for bryophyte and vascular plant species.

OSE pad preparation modifies banks of vegetative propagules and seeds, exposes lower layers of non-decomposed peat, and reduces variation in microtopography and microhabitats available for bryophyte and vascular plant species.

Loss of vertical microtopography and prolonged surface flooding after OSE operations were associated with major shifts in vegetation composition away from reference habitat conditions and will need to be managed.

Loss of vertical microtopography and prolonged surface flooding after OSE operations were associated with major shifts in vegetation composition away from reference habitat conditions and will need to be managed.

Loss of vertical microtopography and prolonged surface flooding after OSE operations were associated with major shifts in vegetation composition away from reference habitat conditions and will need to be managed.
As flooding in summer is detrimental to most boreal forest trees (Grossnickle 2000), tree seedlings are unlikely to survive in the bryophyte-poor OSE habitat.
As flooding in summer is detrimental to most boreal forest trees (Grossnickle 2000), tree seedlings are unlikely to survive in the bryophyte-poor OSE habitat.
is expected to greatly increase the length of time it takes for WMR fens affected by OSE drilling to reestablish vegetation typical of undisturbed habitat.
mean total sedge cover per plot was significantly higher in OSE habitat (bryophyte-poor=44.7%; bryophyte-dominated=37.6%) than references (bryophyte-poor=3.1%; bryophyte-dominated =5.6%)

excavating with machinery, large intact mounds or blocks of peat after drilling operations in the winter when the peat surface is frozen. These could create microsites that are elevated above the water table surface during high water levels, for colonization of woody plants and hummock-building mosses

excavating with machinery, large intact mounds or blocks of peat after drilling operations in the winter when the peat surface is frozen. These could create microsites that are elevated above the water table surface during high water levels, for colonization of woody plants and hummock-building mosses
Minimizing disturbance severity during OSE site preparation may limit the need for active restoration measures.
Minimizing disturbance severity during OSE site preparation may limit the need for active restoration measures.
Flooding has also been shown to affect the establishment of some moss species negatively
mean total sedge cover per plot was significantly higher in OSE habitat (bryophyte-poor=44.7%; bryophyte-dominated=37.6%) than references (bryophyte-poor=3.1%; bryophyte-dominated =5.6%)
